title: senecillis jaluensis
parts: Senecillis jaluensis has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, flowers, roots, and stems. The leaves of Senecillis jaluensis are known to contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids, alkaloids, and sesquiterpenes, which may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots of the plant contain saponins, which have been traditionally used to treat fever and skin conditions, while the flowers are rich in essential oils and volatile compounds with potential antimicrobial and antifungal effects. Additionally, the stems of Senecillis jaluensis may contain terpenoids, which have been reported to exhibit antiviral and anti-inflammatory activities.
side_effects: Senecillis jaluensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing liver damage and kidney failure due to the presence of pyrrolizidine alkaloids. Prolonged consumption of the plant can lead to symptoms like jaundice, abdominal pain, and fatigue. In severe cases, it can cause pulmonary hypertension and cardiac problems. If not handled carefully, the plant's toxins can accumulate in the body and cause irreversible damage.
